As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```

6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard

As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```

 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```

 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard

#5469 
As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```
(lf) harshil-shah@harshil-shah-HP-Pavilion-Laptop-15-eg2xxx:~/lfortran$ src/bin/lfortran tests/errors/max_01.f90
warning: Different kinds of args in max0 is a non-standard extension
 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

#5469 
As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```
(lf) harshil-shah@harshil-shah-HP-Pavilion-Laptop-15-eg2xxx:~/lfortran$ src/bin/lfortran tests/errors/max_01.f90
warning: Different kinds of args in max0 is a non-standard extension
 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

#5469 
As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```
(lf) harshil-shah@harshil-shah-HP-Pavilion-Laptop-15-eg2xxx:~/lfortran$ src/bin/lfortran tests/errors/max_01.f90
warning: Different kinds of args in max0 is a non-standard extension
 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
9.00000000000000000e+00

As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```
(lf) harshil-shah@harshil-shah-HP-Pavilion-Laptop-15-eg2xxx:~/lfortran$ src/bin/lfortran tests/errors/max_01.f90
warning: Different kinds of args in max0 is a non-standard extension
 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```

 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard

https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

#5469 
As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```
(lf) harshil-shah@harshil-shah-HP-Pavilion-Laptop-15-eg2xxx:~/lfortran$ src/bin/lfortran tests/errors/max_01.f90
warning: Different kinds of args in max0 is a non-standard extension
 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

#5469 
As discussed in the issue, the code now gives a warning but executes the max and min correctly.
```fortran
program max_01
    real(8) :: y
    real :: z
    y = 5.2d0
    z = 9.0
    print *, max(y, z)
end program
```
```
(lf) harshil-shah@harshil-shah-HP-Pavilion-Laptop-15-eg2xxx:~/lfortran$ src/bin/lfortran tests/errors/max_01.f90
warning: Different kinds of args in max0 is a non-standard extension
 --> tests/errors/max_01.f90:6:14
  |
6 |     print *, max(y, z)
  |              ^^^^^^^^^ help: ensure all arguments have the same kind to make it standard


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
9.00000000000000000e+00
```

The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8

#4629 
The real function is updated so that if a kind is specified, the output is of that kind, otherwise the same kind as the input. 

For
```
program main
    complex(8) :: arr2(3) = [(5._8, 7._8), (8.2_8, 7.12_8), (1.2_8, 3.4_8)]
    print *, kind(real(arr2))
end program 
```

Output
```
$ lfortran a.f90
8
